pi_items.metadata_value: Multimodality CT+MRI correlation for joint pain assessment
pi_items.verified_value: Multimodality CT+MRI correlation for joint pain assessment
pi_items.source: task.json description describes this exact scenario (evaluating a patient with chronic joint pain and limited mobility using CT and MRI)
task.json description and verifier.py scoring logic explicitly check for side-by-side layouts, text reports, distinct measurements, exported images, and modality terms

privileged_info_summary: The task instructions establish a clinical scenario of a PM&R physician comparing CT and MRI scans. The required directories (/home/ga/DICOM/studies/pmr_ct/ and /home/ga/DICOM/studies/pmr_mri/) and output paths (/home/ga/DICOM/exports/comparison_view.png and comparison_report.txt) are strictly enforced by the setup and export scripts. The features evaluated by the verifier include multi-series loading, side-by-side layout, dual window/level settings, measurements, image export, and a text report. However, data analysis reveals a discrepancy: while the task description specifies using CT and MRI data, the downloaded datasets from rubomedical.com (dicom_viewer_0002.zip and dicom_viewer_0003.zip) both actually contain X-Ray Angiography (XA) series for a 'Rubo DEMO' patient, making the underlying data real but misaligned with the stated modalities.
